document_title: SERIES A PREFERRED STOCK PURCHASE AGREEMENT

dates.source_text: THIS SERIES A PREFERRED STOCK PURCHASE AGREEMENT (this “Agreement”), is made as of [●], 2025, by and among A Inc., a Delaware corporation (the “Company”), the investors listed on Exhibit A attached to this Agreement (each a “SAFE Purchaser” and together the “SAFE Purchasers”) and the investors listed on Exhibit B attached to this Agreement (each a “Cash Purchaser” and together the “Cash Purchasers”; the SAFE Purchasers and Cash Purchasers shall collectively be referred to in this Agreement as the “Purchasers”, and each a “Purchaser”).

If, on or prior to [●], 2026(the “Tranche 1 Deadline”), the Company achieves the milestone set forth on Exhibit C attached hereto (the “Tranche 1 Closing Milestone,” and the requirement to achieve such Tranche 1 Milestone, the “Tranche 1 Closing Condition”), and following written notice (the “Milestone Notice”) thereof to the Cash Purchasers, the Cash Purchasers holding a majority of the then-outstanding shares of Series A-2 Preferred Stock purchased at the Initial Closing (the “Requisite Cash Purchasers”) either confirm in writing that such Tranche 1 Closing Condition has been satisfied or...

None of the Company and any of its officers, Key Employees, or agents acting for or on behalf of the Company, has committed any act, made any statement or failed to make any statement, relating to any Company Product that would reasonably be expected to provide a basis for the FDA to invoke its policy with respect to “Fraud, Untrue Statements of Material Facts, Bribery, and Illegal Gratuities” set forth in 56 Fed. Reg. 46191 (September 10, 1991) and any amendments thereto. None of the Company, its officers, Key Employees and agents has been convicted of any crime or engaged in any conduct t...

money.source_text: Subject to the terms and conditions of this Agreement, each SAFE Purchaser agrees to purchase at the Initial Closing (as defined below), and the Company agrees to sell and issue to each SAFE Purchaser at the Initial Closing, shares of Series A-1 Preferred Stock, $0.0001 par value per share (the “Series A-1 Preferred Stock”), at a purchase price of $1.6754 per share, in the number and for the aggregate purchase price set forth opposite each SAFE Purchaser’s name on Exhibit A hereto;

Subject to the terms and conditions of this Agreement, each SAFE Purchaser agrees to purchase at the Initial Closing (as defined below), and the Company agrees to sell and issue to each SAFE Purchaser at the Initial Closing, shares of Series A-1 Preferred Stock, $0.0001 par value per share (the “Series A-1 Preferred Stock”), at a purchase price of $1.6754 per share, in the number and for the aggregate purchase price set forth opposite each SAFE Purchaser’s name on Exhibit A hereto;

Subject to the terms and conditions of this Agreement, each Cash Purchaser agrees to purchase at the applicable Closing (as defined below), and the Company agrees to sell and issue to each Cash Purchaser at the applicable Closing, that number of shares of Series A-2 Preferred Stock, $0.0001 par value per share (the “Series A-2 Preferred Stock”), or Series A-3 Preferred Stock, $0.0001 par value per share (the “Series A-3 Preferred Stock” and together with the Series A-1 Preferred Stock and Series A-2 Preferred Stock, the “Series A Preferred Stock”), as applicable, set forth opposite such Pur...

The Company and each SAFE Purchaser holding a SAFE hereby agree that such SAFE Purchaser’s all SAFEs hereby are and will be deemed for all purposes to have been amended and modified by virtue hereof to the full extent necessary to permit and facilitate their conversion as provided in this Agreement into Convertible Security Shares, to fix the conversion price at $1.6413 per share, and, immediately upon the Initial Closing, such SAFEs shall be deemed terminated in full and null, void and of no further force or effect; provided that the foregoing will not impair the right of the holder of a S...

20,846,398 shares of common stock, $0.0001 par value per share (the “Common Stock”), 5,840,742 shares of which are issued and outstanding immediately prior to the Initial Closing. All of the outstanding shares of Common Stock have been duly authorized, are fully paid and nonassessable and were issued in compliance with all applicable federal and state securities laws.

Except for the Transaction Agreements, there are no agreements, understandings, instruments, contracts or proposed transactions to which the Company is a party or by which it is bound that involve (i) obligations (contingent or otherwise) of, or payments to, the Company in excess of $75,000, (ii) the license of any patent, copyright, trademark, trade secret or other proprietary right to or from the Company, (iii) the grant of rights to manufacture, produce, assemble, license, market, or sell its products to any other Person that limit the Company’s exclusive right to develop, manufacture, a...

The Company has not (i) declared or paid any dividends, or authorized or made any distribution upon or with respect to any class or series of its capital stock, (ii) incurred any indebtedness for money borrowed or incurred any other liabilities individually in excess of $50,000 or in excess of $100,000 in the aggregate, (iii) made any loans or advances to any Person, other than ordinary advances for business expenses, or (iv) sold, exchanged or otherwise disposed of any of its assets or rights, other than in the ordinary course of business. For the purposes of (a) and (b) of this Section 2....

Remedies. In the event any Cash Purchaser incurs over $50,000 in aggregate losses, damages, costs (including the outside attorneys’ fees and court costs), penalties, interest, expenses, assessments and/or fines (collectively “Losses”) following the date of this Agreement (for the avoidance of doubt, in no event shall any of the following be considered Losses incurred by a Cash Purchaser pursuant to this Section 8.1: (i) a subsequent financing conducted by the Company at a reduced valuation, or (ii) the issuance of capital stock or deemed issuance of capital stock by the Company at price per...

Fees and Expenses. Within five business days after the later of (a) the receipt of a summary invoice therefor or (b) the Initial Closing, the Company shall pay the reasonable fees and expenses of, the counsel for B, in an amount not to exceed, in the aggregate, $60,000.

percentages.source_text: Sanctions. (a) During the last five (5) years, the Company and its subsidiaries have complied with applicable laws and regulations pertaining to trade and economic sanctions administered by the United States (“Sanctions”). (b) None of the Company, its subsidiaries, or their respective directors, officers, employees, or, to the Company’s knowledge, the Company’s or subsidiaries’ agents is: (i) organized under the laws of, ordinarily resident in, or located in a country or territory that is the subject of comprehensive Sanctions (“Restricted Countries”); (ii) 50% or more owned by the governme...

Ten First-In-Man (FIM) patients are enrolled, and there are results for no less than one month, with the incidence of Serious Adverse Events (SAE) not exceeding 10%, unless waived by the Requisite Cash Purchasers. Serious Adverse Event shall mean an event that results in death, is life-threatening, requires or prolongs hospitalization, causes disability/incapacity or congenital anomaly, or requires intervention to prevent such outcomes where such event is reasonably attributed to the Company Product.
